title: A Good Walk Ashore
summary: Beaufort’s waterfront and downtown are one of the town’s real strengths for cruisers.
display_text: Beaufort rewards crews who make time to step ashore. The waterfront park and historic downtown give this stop more charm than a simple in-and-out marina night.
audio_text: Beaufort is one of those places where stepping ashore is part of the value. The waterfront and historic downtown make a strong case for slowing down a little.

title: Watch the Current
summary: A practical marina note for Beaufort arrivals.
display_text: Around Beaufort, current deserves respect at docking time. That is especially true at practical transient stops where timing can make the difference between tidy and theatrical.
audio_text: In Beaufort, current is worth taking seriously when you dock. The boat may prefer good timing even more than the crew does.
source_credit: Source: Waterway Guide practical marina notes
notes: Practical current-handling note for Beaufort area.

title: More Than a Fuel Stop
summary: Beaufort has enough character to justify extra time.
display_text: Beaufort is not just a place to top off and move on. Between the historic district, waterfront setting, and easy-town feel, it can be a morale-improving stop for a cruising crew.
audio_text: Beaufort has enough charm to turn a practical stop into a pleasant one. That is not a bad quality in a town.

title: Useful, Not Just Pretty
summary: Beaufort combines practical stop value with a good ashore experience.
display_text: Beaufort works well when you want a stop that feels pleasant without giving up practical value. That blend of utility and charm is one reason cruisers remember it.
audio_text: Beaufort manages a useful trick. It can be both practical and pleasant, which is more than some stops can claim.
source_credit: Source: CruisersNet local marina and stopover context
